exercises.title: Trace try-catch basic flow
exercises.scenario: Exception thrown mid-try — remaining lines skipped.
hints: 'before' prints before the exception
parseInt('bad') throws NumberFormatException
'after' is SKIPPED — after throw point
'caught' runs in catch block
'done' runs after the try-catch

exercises.title: Trace try-catch-finally
hints: try: 'try' prints, arr[5] throws
catch: exception caught, 'catch' prints
finally: ALWAYS runs
after: execution continues

exercises.title: Safe parseInt
exercises.scenario: Wrap a parse operation in try-catch.
exercises.instructions:
Write a method `safeParseInt(String s)` that returns the parsed integer, or -1 if parsing fails. Test with '42', 'abc'.

Expected output:
42
-1
exercises.starter_code: // safeParseInt: returns parsed int or -1 on failure
hints: try { return Integer.parseInt(s); }
catch (NumberFormatException e) { return -1; }

exercises.title: Fix wrong catch order
exercises.scenario: Parent exception catches everything — specific catch never reached.
exercises.starter_code: // Fix the catch order so specific exception is caught first

bugs.issue: catch(Exception) before catch(NumberFormatException) — specific is unreachable

hints: Specific (child) exceptions FIRST
General (parent) exceptions LAST
NumberFormatException extends RuntimeException extends Exception

exercises.title: Multiple catch blocks
exercises.scenario: Different exceptions caught and handled differently.
exercises.instructions:
Write `safeDivide(String a, String b)` that returns the division result as a String, or an appropriate error message:
- If either isn't a number: 'Error: invalid number'
- If b is zero: 'Error: division by zero'

Test with: ('10','2'), ('10','abc'), ('10','0')

Expected output:
5
Error: invalid number
Error: division by zero
exercises.starter_code: // safeDivide with two catch blocks for two exception types

test_cases.expected_stdout:
5
Error: invalid number
Error: division by zero

hints: Parse both strings first — NumberFormatException if either fails
Then divide — ArithmeticException if b is 0
Two separate catch blocks, each with specific message

exercises.title: Finally for cleanup
exercises.scenario: Simulate resource cleanup guaranteed by finally.
exercises.instructions:
Simulate opening/closing a DB connection. Use finally to ensure 'Connection closed' ALWAYS prints, even if an exception occurs.

Test two cases: normal query, failing query.

Expected output:
Connection opened
Query executed: SELECT * FROM users
Connection closed
---
Connection opened
Error: Query failed
Connection closed
exercises.starter_code: // Simulate DB connection with finally-based cleanup

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Connection opened
Query executed: SELECT * FROM users
Connection closed
---
Connection opened
Error: Query failed
Connection closed

hints: println 'Connection opened' before try
finally block: println 'Connection closed'
finally runs whether exception thrown or not

exercises.title: Finally with return
exercises.scenario: Finally runs even when try has a return.
exercises.expected_solution:
finally runs
ok
finally runs
caught

test_cases.expected:
finally runs
ok
finally runs
caught

hints: test(false): no exception → 'return ok' starts → finally runs → returns 'ok'
test(true): exception thrown → catch returns 'caught' → finally runs first → returns 'caught'
finally ALWAYS runs before return

exercises.title: Multi-catch with | operator
exercises.scenario: Same handling for multiple exception types.
exercises.instructions:
Write `parseAndAccess(String indexStr, String[] array)` that:
- Parses indexStr as int (NumberFormatException possible)
- Accesses array[index] (ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException possible)
- Returns the value, or an error message
- Uses multi-catch (|) since both errors get the same message: 'Invalid input'

Expected output:
hello
Invalid input
Invalid input
hints: catch (NumberFormatException | ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException e)
Both get the same message — multi-catch is cleaner than two blocks
Same exception variable e works for both types

exercises.title: Exception getMessage and class
exercises.scenario: Read exception details from the exception object.
exercises.instructions:
Write `describeException(Runnable action)` that runs the action in a try-catch, and if an exception occurs, prints:
- The exception class (simple name)
- The message

Test with three Runnables that throw different exceptions.

Expected output:
ArithmeticException: / by zero
NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "String.length()" because "s" is null
NumberFormatException: For input string: "xyz"
exercises.starter_code: // describeException prints class + message of any caught exception
hints: e.getClass().getSimpleName() gives just the class name (no package)
e.getMessage() gives the description
Runnable is a functional interface with run() — accepts lambdas

exercises.title: Robust input parser
exercises.scenario: Parse multiple fields with individual error messages.
exercises.instructions:
Parse a CSV line 'name,age,salary' into a Person object. Return descriptive error if any field is wrong.

Test: 'Aisha,28,75000', 'Raj,notAnAge,85000', 'Priya,25,'

Expected output:
Person: Aisha, 28, ₹75000.0
Error: Age must be a valid integer
Error: Salary must be a valid number
exercises.starter_code: // Parse CSV line with field-specific error messages

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Person: Aisha, 28, ₹75000.0
Error: Age must be a valid integer
Error: Salary must be a valid number

hints: Separate try-catch for age and salary — different error messages per field
Parsing a blank string '' as double throws NumberFormatException
record is a Java 16+ shorthand for immutable data class

exercises.title: Retry logic with exception handling
exercises.scenario: Retry on failure — common production pattern.
exercises.instructions:
Implement `retryParse(String[] attempts, int maxRetries)` — tries to parse each string as int, returns the first successful parse, stops after maxRetries total attempts.

Input: ['abc', 'def', '42', '99'], maxRetries=3

Expected output:
Attempt 1: 'abc' — failed
Attempt 2: 'def' — failed
Attempt 3: '42' — success: 42
exercises.starter_code: // Retry parse: try each until success or maxRetries exhausted

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Attempt 1: 'abc' — failed
Attempt 2: 'def' — failed
Attempt 3: '42' — success: 42

hints: Loop up to maxRetries, try parseInt each time
On success: print and return
On NumberFormatException: print failed, continue loop

exercises.title: Bank transfer with exception handling
exercises.instructions:
Implement `transfer(Map<String, Double> accounts, String from, String to, String amountStr)`.

Errors:
- Amount not a number → 'Invalid amount format'
- Amount <= 0 → 'Amount must be positive'
- From account doesn't exist → 'Account not found: [from]'
- Insufficient funds → 'Insufficient funds in [from]'

OnSuccess: update balances, return 'Transfer complete'

Expected output:
Transfer complete
Insufficient funds in A001
Invalid amount format
Account not found: A999
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;

// Bank transfer with comprehensive error handling

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Transfer complete
Insufficient funds in A001
Invalid amount format
Account not found: A999

hints: Parse amount first in try-catch
Then validate: amount > 0, accounts exist, sufficient balance
After first transfer: A001 has 3000 (was 5000, transferred 2000)

exercises.title: Exception propagation demo
exercises.scenario: Show how exceptions travel through the call stack.
exercises.instructions:
Create a 3-level call chain where the deepest method throws, the middle method catches and rethrows as RuntimeException, and the top catches the RuntimeException. Print the chain.

Expected output:
deepMethod: throwing
middleMethod: caught, rethrowing as RuntimeException
topMethod: caught RuntimeException: original cause was NumberFormatException

test_cases.expected_stdout:
deepMethod: throwing
middleMethod: caught, rethrowing as RuntimeException
topMethod: caught RuntimeException: original cause was NumberFormatException

hints: deepMethod: throws NumberFormatException via parseInt
middleMethod: catch NFE, throw new RuntimeException('wrapped', e)
topMethod: catch RuntimeException, use e.getCause().getClass().getSimpleName()

exercises.title: Robust file-like processor with finally
exercises.scenario: Resource cleanup guaranteed by finally — real production pattern.
exercises.instructions:
Simulate a transaction processor: open → process N items → close. Items can fail individually. Use finally to guarantee 'closed' even if a catastrophic error occurs mid-processing.

Items: [100, 200, 'bad', 300, 0] — 'bad' fails to parse, 0 triggers ArithmeticException (divide by zero in processing)

Expected output:
Processor opened
Processed: 100
Processed: 200
Skipped invalid item: bad
Processed: 300
Skipped invalid item: 0 (/ by zero)
Processor closed
Total processed: 3

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Processor opened
Processed: 100
Processed: 200
Skipped invalid item: bad
Processed: 300
Skipped invalid item: 0 (/ by zero)
Processor closed
Total processed: 3

hints: Outer try/finally: processor lifecycle
Inner try/catch per item: handle individual failures without stopping the batch
finally closes regardless of what happens inside
1000/0 throws ArithmeticException with message '/ by zero'

exercises.title: Complete payment form validator
exercises.scenario: Full real-world form validation with specific exception handling per field.
exercises.instructions:
Build a payment form validator for a Razorpay-style checkout:

Fields: cardNumber (16 digits), expiryYear (4-digit int, 2024-2030), cvv (3-digit int), amount (positive double)

Validate and parse each field. Return a FULL validation report listing all errors (not just the first).

Test with: cardNumber='1234567890123456', expiryYear='2028', cvv='12x', amount='-500'

Expected output:
Errors:
- CVV must be a 3-digit number
- Amount must be positive
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;

// Payment form validator — collects ALL errors, not just first

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Errors:
- CVV must be a 3-digit number
- Amount must be positive

hints: Collect ALL errors in a List<String> — don't return on first error
Each field has its own try-catch for NumberFormatException
After parsing: validate the value (range, length) with if-checks
'12x' fails parseInt → 'CVV must be a 3-digit number'
'-500' parses to -500.0 but is <= 0 → 'Amount must be positive'
